title: Low quiescent current load switch
abstract: A load switch circuit (100) includes a driver (112) to control a gate (114) of a transistor. The gate (114) enables the transistor to deliver power to a load from a power supply (130) when the gate (114) is activated. A gate slope control (116) controls a rate of change over time of a voltage associated with the gate (114) to activate the gate (114) and to disable the driver (112) when the gate (114) is activated.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A load switch circuit comprising: a transistor including a gate, a source and a drain; a transconductor connected to the gate of the transistor; a comparator connected to an input of the trans conductor; and a resistor including a first terminal and a second terminal, the first terminal connected to the gate of the transistor, and the second terminal connected to at least one of a charge pump or ground. 2. The circuit of claim 1, wherein: the trans conductor is to convert a voltage to a current input for the transistor gate; the current input to the transistor gate is to control the transistor gate to deliver power to a load from a power supply; and the resistor is to provide power from a charge pump to the transistor gate as controlled by the transconductor. 3. The circuit of claim 1, wherein the comparator is to disable the transconductor when a voltage of the transistor gate reaches a charge pump voltage. 4. The circuit of claim 1, further including a latch connected to the comparator. 5. The circuit of claim 4, wherein the latch is to store and provide a signal to disable the transconductor. 6. The circuit of claim 1, further including a capacitor connected to the trans conductor. 7. The circuit of claim 1, wherein the capacitor is to provide the voltage to the transconductor. 8. The circuit of claim 1, wherein the transconductor and comparator are to be implemented using N-type metal-oxide-semiconductor logic components. 9. An apparatus comprising: a driver to control a gate of a transistor, the gate to enable the transistor deliver power to a load from a power supply when the gate is activated; and a gate slope control circuit to control a rate of change over time of a voltage associated with the gate to activate the gate and to disable the driver when the gate is activated. 10. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the driver includes a transconductor to convert a voltage to a current to be inputted to the gate. 11. The apparatus of claim 10, wherein the driver further includes a capacitor to provide the voltage to the transconductor. 12. The apparatus of claim 10, wherein the gate slope control is to pull up the voltage to an input voltage when gate slope control is to disable the driver. 13. The apparatus of claim 10, wherein the gate slope control includes a comparator to detect when the voltage satisfies a threshold and to disable the driver when the voltage satisfies the threshold. 14. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the driver further includes a charge pump and a resistor to provide power to the gate. 15. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the gate slope control further includes a latch to store and provide a signal to disable the driver. 16. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the driver and the gate slope control are to be implemented using N-type metal-oxide-semiconductor logic components. 17. A system comprising: a power supply to provide a voltage; a gate slope control and driver circuit to receive and convert the voltage to a current to activate a gate of a transistor to provide power from the power supply; and a device to receive the power from the power supply when the gate slope control and driver circuit activates the gate, wherein the gate slope control and driver circuit is to control activation of the gate at a rate of change over time corresponding to a rate of change in the current over time until the gate is activated and to disable the gate slope control and driver circuit when the gate is activated. 18. The system of claim 17, wherein the driver of the gate slope control and driver circuit includes a transconductor to convert the voltage to a current input to the gate, and the gate slope control and driver circuit includes a comparator to control activation of the gate and to disable the driver when the controlled slope is activated. 19. The system of claim 18, wherein the gate slope control and driver circuit is to pull up the voltage to an input voltage when the comparator is to disable the driver. 20. The system of claim 18, wherein the gate slope control and driver circuit further includes a charge pump and a resistor to provide power to the gate. 21. The system of claim 17, wherein the power supply includes a capacitor to provide the voltage to the gate slope control and driver circuit. 22. The system of claim 17, wherein the gate slope control and driver circuit is to be implemented using N-type metal-oxide-semiconductor logic components.
